expectations.text: Treats the prompt as under-specified — either asks the user for the missing keys OR shows discovery code that enumerates candidates first
expectations.evidence: Says 'Your prompt is under-specified' and provides Step 1 'Enumerate populated LFP rows' via `LFPSelection.fetch('KEY', as_dict=True)`, plus a 'What I need from you to give you the one-shot version' section.

expectations.text: Names the LFP-specific keys the user needs to choose (`lfp_electrode_group_name`, `target_interval_list_name`, `filter_name`, `filter_sampling_rate`)
expectations.evidence: Lists all four: lfp_electrode_group_name, target_interval_list_name, filter_name, filter_sampling_rate.

expectations.text: Resolves the merge_id through a merge-aware helper (`LFPOutput.merge_get_part(...).fetch1('KEY')` OR `LFPOutput.merge_restrict(...)`), NOT through a bare `LFPOutput & {nwb_file_name: ...}` restriction
expectations.evidence: Uses `LFPOutput.merge_get_part(chosen_key).fetch1('KEY')` and explicitly warns against `(LFPOutput & {'nwb_file_name': ...})` bare restrictions.

expectations.text: Does NOT hand back a single-line bare-restriction `fetch1_dataframe()` without first verifying cardinality or scoping
expectations.evidence: Includes explicit `assert len(sel) == 1` cardinality check before the fetch1, and the bare-restriction shape is shown only as anti-pattern.
